title: Q1 Results 17th July Live: Reliance shares up ahead of results, JSW Steel, Havells, Oberoi Realty, Tata Tech to announce Q1 results, Jio Financial, Tech Mahindra, BHEL soar after Q1, Wipro, WeWork, CE
text:
Business people using pen,tablet,notebook are planning a marketing plan to improve the quality of their sales in the future. | Photo Credit: Jirapong Manustrong

Q1 Results Today, 17th July 2026 Live Updates: Find all the latest Q1 results 2026 updates of Reliance Industries, JSW Steel, Federal Bank, Havells India, Oberoi Realty, Poonawalla Fincorp, Tata Technologies, Central Bank of India, RBL Bank, Jayaswal Neco Industries, Turtlemint Fintech Solutions, Tatva Chintan Pharma Chem, Globus Spirits, Navkar Corporation, Amal, Pil Italica Lifestyle, Radhe Developers (India), Lakhotia Polyesters, Nam Securities, Vivimed Labs, B2B Software Technologies, TOYAM SPORTS, Lake Shore Realty, and COSYN

Reliance shares were up 2% to Rs 1,321.40 on the NSE at 11.27 am, hitting a high of Rs 1323.70 from the previous close of Rs 1,296.60.

The stock traded at ₹3,550.40 on the NSE at 10.20 am after falling to an intraday low of ₹3,471.10 from the previous close of ₹3,829.60

Newgen Software shares swing, trading 1.3% lower at Rs 528.60 on the NSE at 10.30 am, after moderating between Rs 524.50 and Rs 561.10.

Its revenue grew 11% y-o-y to Rs 357 crore in Q1FY27, while profit after tax increased 26% y-o-y to Rs 63 crore.

WeWork India shares traded 5% lower at Rs 692.65 on the NSE at 10.27 am, hitting a low of Rs 658 from the previous close of Rs 728.05.

It reported a consolidated net loss of Rs 4.30 crore in Q1FY27 as against the net loss of Rs 14.10 crore in the year-ago period.

Jio Financial Services shares surged 6 per cent on Friday after the company reported a sharp rise in first-quarter earnings, driven by strong growth in interest income, fees and commission income, and higher dividend income.

Polycab India shares declined 3.5% to Rs 8885 from the previous close of Rs 9,215.

It reported net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 766.27 crore compared to Rs 564.9 crore in the same quarter last year

Borosil Renewables Limited Q1FY27 Results:-

EBITDA Margin 15.36% vs 14.55% YoY & 13.95% QoQ

Other Income 1.32 Cr vs 1.24 Cr YoY & 0.50 Cr QoQ

BHEL shares rose 2.5% to a fresh 52-week high of Rs 446.50 on the NSE.

It posted a consolidated net profit of ₹376.71 crore in the June quarter,against loss recorded in the corresponding quarter.

ITC Hotels stock traded flat at Rs 175.40 at 10.05 am. Its consolidated net profit rose 36 per cent to ₹182 crore in Q1FY27.

Jefferies maintained its underperform rating on the stock and cut its target price to ₹150 from ₹180 and said another soft quarter and September 2026 quarter guidance implied a delayed recovery

Tech Mahindra shares surged 3% to Rs 1,551.70 on the NSE at 9.35 am, hitting a high of Rs 1,562.90.

Tech Mahindra posted a 17.6 per cent year-on-year rise in revenue at ₹15,711 crore. Net profit stood at ₹1,486 crore, up 31.6 per cent year-on-year.

Wipro shares were down 2% to Rs 174.20 on the NSE at 9.29 am after reporting a net profit of ₹3,352 crore Q1FY27.

Jio Financial shares traded 4% higher at Rs 245.95 on the NSE at 9.27 am, hitting a high of Rs 249.95 from the previous close of Rs 235.65. Its consolidated first quarter (Q1FY27) net profit zoomed 156 per cent y-o-y to ₹830 crore.

WeWork India Management Limited Q1FY27 Results:-

EBITDA Margin 64.05% vs 62.72% YoY & 64.67% QoQ

PBT -3.94 Cr vs -14.10 Cr YoY & +44.01 Cr QoQ

PAT -4.06 Cr vs -14.15 Cr YoY & +65.87 Cr QoQ

-4.31 Cr vs -14.10 Cr YoY & +65.55 Cr QoQ

Other Income (Including Finance income) 16.91 Cr vs 10.40 Cr YoY & 19.29 Cr QoQ

Sensex gained 424.69 pts or 0.55% to 77,611.56 at 9.17 am after opening at 77,370.77 from the previous close of 77,186.87; Nifty 50 increased 102.75 pts or 0.43% to 24,175.50.

Tech Mahindra surpasses Wipro in Q1 with 17.6% revenue growth and 31.6% profit increase amid Wipro's profitability struggles.

Wipro's Q1 report shows mixed results: revenue up, margins down, and cautious guidance amid evolving client spending.

Heritage Foods reports Q1 net profit dip to ₹25 crore despite 18% revenue growth to ₹1,338 crore amid rising milk costs.

CEAT reports 22% revenue growth in Q1 FY27, but profit plummets due to raw-material costs and finance expenses.
